<!-- This reference reflects rimsky v0.8.0 — the `reconciledAgainst` release in .claude-plugin/plugin.json. -->

# Claim Producer

Source: `lib/protocols/proto/v1/claim_producer.proto`

## Services

### ClaimProducer

ClaimProducer is the wire-form rimsky↔producer contract. Every
standard claim-producer binary implements this interface; rimsky's
runtime/peer/ package implements the gRPC client.

Lifecycle events live in the separate LifecycleSubscriber service
(lifecycle.proto). A binary that wishes to react to control-plane
events implements both services.

#### ClaimProducer.Capabilities

- Request: `CapabilitiesRequest`
- Response: `CapabilitiesResponse`

Capabilities is the startup-handshake verb. Called once per
producer per rimsky process at startup; the result is cached
and validated against the operator's declared requirements. Not
called on the runtime path.

#### ClaimProducer.Open

- Request: `OpenRequest`
- Response: `OpenResponse`

Open creates whatever producer-internal state the (intent,
realized write_semantics) combination requires and returns the
producer-supplied address the executor will use on the data path.
Called inside the rimsky-side acquisition transaction; the producer
runs its own state mutation in its own transaction.

#### ClaimProducer.Commit

- Request: `CommitRequest`
- Response: `CommitResponse`

Commit signals that the consumer of the claim succeeded. The
producer decides what to do with its own state per its own
configuration.

#### ClaimProducer.Abandon

- Request: `AbandonRequest`
- Response: `AbandonResponse`

Abandon signals that the consumer of the claim failed. The
producer decides what to do with its own state per its own
configuration.

#### ClaimProducer.Release

- Request: `ReleaseRequest`
- Response: `ReleaseResponse`

Release tears down producer-internal read state created at Open.
For producers that registered no per-claim state, typically a no-op.

#### ClaimProducer.SplitScope

- Request: `SplitScopeRequest`
- Response: `SplitScopeResponse`

SplitScope is optional. Producers that advertise
CapabilitiesResponse.supports_split_scope = true MUST implement
this RPC. The supervisor calls it inside the rimsky-side
acquisition transaction for fan-out nodes: the parent claim is
already Open'd, then SplitScope partitions the parent scope into
sub-scope descriptors used to seed sub-claim rows. The shape of
partition_request bytes is producer-defined.

#### ClaimProducer.ScopesConflict

- Request: `ClaimScopesConflictRequest`
- Response: `ScopesConflictResponse`

ScopesConflict is optional. Producers that advertise
CapabilitiesResponse.supports_scopes_conflict = true MUST
implement this RPC. When unsupported, rimsky falls back to the
byte-equal default (per @blessed-invariant 4b).

## Messages

### CapabilitiesRequest

### CapabilitiesResponse

CapabilitiesResponse advertises the set of WriteSemantics values the
producer may realize on Open. Operator config (rimsky.yml's
write_semantics_allowed: per claim_producers entry) declares a
permitted subset that MUST be a subset of this set; rimsky validates
strict subset at startup.

protocols advertises the mix-in service protocols this binary
implements alongside ClaimProducer (e.g. "data_processing",
"validation", "lifecycle_subscriber"). validation_supported_roles
lists the role discriminators this service is willing to validate
when it advertises the "validation" protocol.

| Field | Type | # | Description |
|-------|------|---|-------------|
| `write_semantics_allowed` | `repeated WriteSemantics` | 1 |  |
| `supports_split_scope` | `bool` | 2 | supports_split_scope: producer implements SplitScope. Required for templates that fan out against this producer; rimsky rejects such templates at registration when the producer advertises false. |
| `supports_scopes_conflict` | `bool` | 3 | supports_scopes_conflict: producer implements ScopesConflict. When false, rimsky uses byte-equal default (per @blessed-invariant 4b). |
| `protocols` | `repeated string` | 4 | protocols advertises the mix-in service protocols this binary implements alongside ClaimProducer (e.g. "data_processing", "validation", "lifecycle_subscriber"). |
| `validation_supported_roles` | `repeated string` | 5 | validation_supported_roles: when "validation" is in protocols, the set of role discriminators this service is willing to validate ("executor" \| "claim_producer" \| "lifecycle_subscriber" \| "sensor"). |

### OpenRequest

OpenRequest carries the rimsky-generated claim_id plus the resolved
claim spec. selector is post-substitution; the producer parses it.
template_id and instance_id form the per-spec scope envelope:
opaque to rimsky, populated from the dispatch row's instance →
template lookup, available to the producer for namespace routing or
trace correlation.

| Field | Type | # | Description |
|-------|------|---|-------------|
| `claim_id` | `string` | 1 |  |
| `producer_name` | `string` | 2 |  |
| `selector` | `string` | 3 |  |
| `intent` | `string` | 4 |  |
| `alias` | `string` | 5 |  |
| `template_id` | `string` | 6 |  |
| `instance_id` | `string` | 7 |  |
| `run_scope_id` | `string` | 8 | run_scope_id is the RunScope this claim lives in; used by the host-agent-proxy to key per-run-scope spawn isolation on the claim-producer path. Opaque to rimsky. |

### OpenResponse

OpenResponse signals acquisition outcome. Producers that always
have a claim to give return Acquired. Producers that may have
nothing right now (e.g. an empty items-table queue) return
Unavailable; rimsky retries on the next scheduler tick. Producer-
side faults flow as gRPC error status codes, not as an Unavailable
response.

| Field | Type | # | Description |
|-------|------|---|-------------|
| `acquired` | `Acquired` | 1 |  |
| `unavailable` | `Unavailable` | 2 |  |

### Acquired

Acquired carries the producer's acquisition outputs. Address,
payload, and scope are opaque bytes per blessed invariant 20;
any or all may be empty depending on the (write_semantics, intent)
combination. realized_write_semantics declares the per-claim
semantics; MUST be a member of the producer's
CapabilitiesResponse.write_semantics_allowed; MUST be uniform
across byte-equal-scope claims (uniformity invariant per spec §2.5).

| Field | Type | # | Description |
|-------|------|---|-------------|
| `address` | `bytes` | 1 |  |
| `payload` | `bytes` | 2 |  |
| `claim_scope` | `bytes` | 3 |  |
| `realized_write_semantics` | `WriteSemantics` | 4 |  |

### Unavailable

Unavailable signals "no claim available right now." The optional
error_class names the producer-declared acquisition-failure class
(a member of the producer's declared error vocabulary, e.g.
"pg/claim_unavailable") rimsky's acquisition-failure routing keys the
operator's `error_types:` chain on. Leaving it empty preserves the
historical behavior: rimsky routes the unavailability under the
synthetic class "acquire/unavailable". The Available=false wire shape
(this Unavailable arm of the oneof) is unchanged; the class is an
out-of-band hint consumed on the rimsky routing side, not a new
acquisition outcome.

| Field | Type | # | Description |
|-------|------|---|-------------|
| `error_class` | `string` | 1 |  |

### CommitResponse

| Field | Type | # | Description |
|-------|------|---|-------------|
| `version_id` | `string` | 1 | Set by DataProcessing-capable producers; opaque to rimsky; persisted in col:rimsky_claim_handles.version_id and table:rimsky_lineage for record_kind: claim_terminal. |
| `producer_metadata` | `bytes` | 2 | Optional producer-supplied metadata bytes. Inert in rimsky per @blessed-invariant 20; surfaced verbatim in the parent run's writeback row at fan-out parent terminal (spec §Output aggregation). |

### ReleaseResponse

### SplitScopeRequest

SplitScopeRequest asks the producer to partition a parent scope.
claim_handle_id is the rimsky-side parent claim_handle id;
partition_request is producer-interpreted bytes (e.g., a date range,
a list of regions, a hash bucket count). Called inside the
rimsky-side acquisition tx for fan-out nodes.

| Field | Type | # | Description |
|-------|------|---|-------------|
| `claim_handle_id` | `string` | 1 |  |
| `partition_request` | `bytes` | 2 |  |

### SubScopeDescriptor

SubScopeDescriptor identifies one of the sub-claim-scopes the producer
partitioned the parent into. claim_scope_data is producer-canonicalized
bytes (same shape rimsky stores on rimsky_claim_handles.claim_scope_data);
partition_key is the human-readable key rimsky persists in
col:rimsky_node_runs.child_key for run-tree bookkeeping;
producer_metadata is opaque per-sub-claim-scope info the producer wants
persisted on the row.

| Field | Type | # | Description |
|-------|------|---|-------------|
| `claim_scope_data` | `bytes` | 1 |  |
| `partition_key` | `string` | 2 |  |
| `producer_metadata` | `bytes` | 3 |  |

### SplitScopeResponse

| Field | Type | # | Description |
|-------|------|---|-------------|
| `sub_scopes` | `repeated SubScopeDescriptor` | 1 |  |

### ClaimScopesConflictRequest

ClaimScopesConflictRequest asks the producer whether two claim_scope_data byte
strings should conflict (single-writer-per-claim-scope serialization).
When unsupported (CapabilitiesResponse.supports_scopes_conflict =
false), rimsky uses byte-equal as the trivial default.

| Field | Type | # | Description |
|-------|------|---|-------------|
| `claim_scope_a` | `bytes` | 1 |  |
| `claim_scope_b` | `bytes` | 2 |  |

### ScopesConflictResponse

| Field | Type | # | Description |
|-------|------|---|-------------|
| `conflicts` | `bool` | 1 |  |

## Enums

### WriteSemantics

WriteSemantics declares how a claim handle's writes coexist with
concurrent claims on byte-equal scopes. Per spec §2.4. UNKNOWN is
the proto-default zero value; producers MUST NOT return it. The
set returned by Capabilities is the allowed values per producer;
each Open returns the realized value for that specific claim.

| Value | # | Description |
|-------|---|-------------|
| `WRITE_SEMANTICS_UNKNOWN` | 0 |  |
| `WRITE_SEMANTICS_SYNC` | 1 |  |
| `WRITE_SEMANTICS_STAGED_ASYNC` | 2 |  |
| `WRITE_SEMANTICS_BLOCKING_ASYNC` | 3 |  |
| `WRITE_SEMANTICS_READ_ONLY` | 4 |  |
